title: Ctenodon histrix

parts: Ctenodon histrix, commonly known as the rough-leafed fig or ctenanthe, is not well-documented in terms of medicinal properties. However, its plant parts, such as leaves and roots, are sometimes used in traditional medicine for various purposes. In some cultures, these plant parts are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. They are also thought to have potential uses in treating fever and digestive issues. More research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Ctenodon histrix for medicinal purposes.
